reviews.review_description: For the time, great. But it's showing its age in 2026. The newer entries are just...better. but fun, it's free so why not?
Okay, I don’t get motion sickness but my brother on the other hand. He kinda released stomach contents.

Honestly the only thing I hated about this game is the cube part. It's very annoying and challenging because you have 2 blocks and you need to go through 3 PLATFORMS, Unlike plants at war and dessert warfare this game doesn't have a story since, well, I mean this game was only meant for entertainment, not story. Honestly I would give this a 6.5/10

I hate the cactus cube things in the first parts of the game I HATE THEM!!!! I understand it is supposed to be challenging but it is annoying the slingshot everywhere when you hit then and the part where you go into a dip then use them to get out sucks I am stuck there with no idea to get out because you can't stack them or nothing and it is just out of jump range
Also is this a game in the lore and since I have not beat it yet I love how the pumpkin guy or whoever from the plants at war game is still alive due to them leaving him there with a flag pole stuck in him they foreshadowed it kinda by amilio or private prickly I forget who saying should we stay with him until he is dead or leave him and we decide to leave him and he survived
The creativity is there but it’s low quality and very challenging.
I love this cactus but I found through a portal glitch that I have no body pls give me a prickly cactus body Also good cactus game . Where is cactus cowboy ww2 game????!??!!??!

developer_response.body: You need to search for "Cactus Cowboy - Plants At War".

reviews.review_description: I love the game portal and portal 2. I know all of the lore. So i was hoping for this game to at least have portals with depth, but it's literally just render textures. But since you can't get portal natively on the quest, i might as well play this.

The graphics and level design are incredibly poor quality, the gameplay is OK but the physics (gravity, mostly) and immersion are not there at all. I can handle relatively intense VR experiences, but the jarring, unrefined motion of the player’s jumping alone was enough to make me want to close my eyes while navigating the levels. If you came here for a Portal clone, you may find some novelty in this game, but it will immediately drive you way due to its aggressively bad presentation.

reviews.review_title: Too snarky for it's own good.
reviews.review_description: I love snark, but this game is very amateurish atm and the combination doesn't play well.
💥This game is so much fun , portal vr cactus style😁💥
Wow it’s really fun

So its like Portal, but with a Cowboy Cactus. It doesn't take itself seriously at all, but still manages to be a challenge and fun. If you ever wondered what portal would be like in VR, you owe it to yourself to try this game. I hope to see other games from the developer eventually, as this game is really fun!

developer_response.body: Very soon! It's gonna be a carnage, cactus style! Check my youtube.

reviews.review_description: It‘s hardcore but extremely fun!
This is portal in VR. I only wish there was a level editor for sharing custom maps.
It’s just portal for the quest 2 natively

a great Portal clone... nan after playing, the story and level design makes it a great game by itself

also if you clip though walls, you can beat the game in plenty of unintended ways, i totally skipped the final boss

also also, this game should have a discord

Rock solid game with the classic portal gun mechanics.  The games version of GlaDOS had me laughing.  Calling me a “cucktus” lol 
Really enjoy the music. 

Hope the Cactus shooter comes to app lab also.

developer_response.body: Very soon, working every minute on the successor.

reviews.review_description: Love the game. It has very fun puzzles!
This is pretty fun. It's simple, easy to get into. Not much here graphically and the portals are just flat images, but I would definitely recommend it.

The cake is still a lie! I was a big fan of Portal, and a big fan of Cactus Cowboy so putting them together is genius. This game has decent puzzles that aren't too hard, but can take some work. It never gets dull, and who doesn't want to be a Cactus Cowboy? Knocked it out of the park yet again
